fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: To support emergency response effectiveness at this property, efforts to manage the dense vegetation could facilitate clearer access routes and enhance visibility for responders. Trimming or strategically clearing some of the vegetation around key areas such as the main building and access pathways may enhance responder navigation and operational efficiency. Additionally, considering the presence of a single access point, ensuring that this entry is kept unobstructed and well-maintained at all times would support smoother responder ingress and egress during emergencies. These actions could also support more effective staging and movement of emergency vehicles and personnel, ensuring rapid and efficient emergency operations throughout the property.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetated Perimeter; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Open Terrain
wildfire.recommendations: Increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure, along with reducing the density of the tree canopy, may significantly improve defensible space and fuel management around the property. Ensuring a clear perimeter around the structure by managing vegetation immediately adjacent to it can reduce potential fire exposure. Pruning or thinning scattered trees and managing vegetation near fences would decrease fuel continuity, thereby supporting fire mitigation efforts. Maintaining the open terrain as a buffer can act as an effective fuel break, limiting the intensity or spread of an approaching fire. Sustained attention to vegetation along the property boundary and within the immediate vicinity of the structure is crucial to enhance long-term wildfire resilience. This can support firefighter access and suppression activities by ensuring clear, accessible routes and reducing the potential for fire to transition quickly to the structures.
